qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype pollution via setOption allows attacker-controlled option injection through Root.fromJSON
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: While this finding correctly identifies protobufjs as the target library and uses Root.fromJSON as an entry point (same as the advisory), the root cause is fundamentally different. The advisory describes arbitrary code execution through unsanitized type names and type references reaching runtime code generation without validation (CWE-94). This finding describes prototype pollution via __proto__ in the options field passing through setOption/setOptions (CWE-1321). The vulnerable code paths differ: the advisory involves the code generation path where type names are interpolated into generated JavaScript; this finding involves the options-handling path in src/object.js setOption. Same library, same entry point API, different root cause and vulnerable code.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The sole qualifying finding (protobufjs-prototype-pollution-setoption) does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory (GHSA-xq3m-2v4x-88gg) describes arbitrary code execution in protobufjs through unsanitized type names and type references reaching runtime code generation when loading crafted JSON descriptors (CWE-94). The finding describes prototype pollution via setOption through __proto__ in options (CWE-1321) in src/object.js, which is a different root cause and vulnerable code path from the advisory's code generation vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory describes CWE-94 code injection through unsanitized type names in code generation from JSON descriptors
Finding targets src/object.js:295 setOption __proto__ handling, not the code generation path
Advisory references patches at commits 535df444ac06 and ff7b2afef875 for code generation sanitization, not setOption options filtering
